name: Reconco Loop from Villena
name_original: Ascenso al Reconco — Circular desde Villena
description: This moderate 31.7-mile road cycling route from Villena features a challenging ascent to Reconco, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villena offers diverse landscapes, from the plains of the Vinalopó Valley to challenging ascents in surrounding mountain ranges like the Sierra de la Villa and Sierra de Salinas. The region features a mix of well-paved roads winding through olive groves, vineyards, and pine forests. This varied terrain provides options for road cyclists of all levels, with routes incorporating both gentle gradients and significant elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Villena The most popular road cycling route is Defending the South Trail, a 37.4 miles (60.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 41 minutes to complete. This route explores the Alto Vinalopó region, passing by historical castles and unique landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Reconco Loop from Villena, a moderate 31.7 miles (51.1 km) path. This route features substantial climbs, offering a challenging experience with panoramic views. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Canalís d'Onil Pass loop from Villena, a 39.0 miles (62.7 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Villena is defined by the Vinalopó Valley, the Sierra de Salinas, and scenic pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Villena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 1800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villena?
A: Villena offers over 50 road cycling routes, providing a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore diverse landscapes, from the Vinalopó Valley to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villena?
A: Yes, Villena has several easy road cycling routes, with 6 specifically categorized as such. The Vinalopó Valley offers flatter terrain, and routes around the Sierra de Salinas Lagoon are known for minimal traffic and low difficulty, making them ideal for those new to road cycling.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Villena?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Villena offers 20 difficult routes with significant elevation gains. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892172513/reconco-loop-from-villena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reconco Loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17321005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Canalís d'Onil Pass loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, leading through mountainous terrain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Area – Biar loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1600 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can families enjoy road cycling in Villena?
A: Yes, Villena offers family-friendly road cycling options. The Vías Verdes, such as the "Vía Verde del Chicharra," are disused railway lines converted into routes suitable for cyclists and walkers, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for families. The area around the Sierra de Salinas Lagoon also features circular routes with minimal traffic and even terrain, perfect for family outings.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Villena?
A: Many of Villena's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892172513/reconco-loop-from-villena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reconco Loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17321005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Biar (810 m) – Canalís d'Onil Pass loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes make planning your ride more convenient.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Villena?
A: Road cycling in Villena offers diverse landscapes. You'll ride through the fertile Vinalopó Valley with its olive groves and vineyards, encounter unique sand formations, and ascend into mountainous areas like the Sierra de la Villa and Sierra de Salinas. Many routes also wind through scenic pine forests, providing varied and picturesque backdrops.

Q: What historical and cultural attractions can I explore along road cycling routes in Villena?
A: Villena's road cycling routes often pass by significant historical and cultural sites. You can explore the Alto Vinalopó region, which features several castles, including the prominent Atalaya Castle in Villena. Other routes might take you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5900085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Sax&lt;/a&gt; or through Villena's historic old town with its notable churches. The Vías Verdes also offer glimpses of archaeological heritage and old train stations.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villena?
A: Villena experiences cool to cold winters and mild to hot summers. While summers can be warm, many months of the year are suitable for cycling. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, allowing you to enjoy the diverse landscapes comfortably.

Q: Is public transport available to access road cycling routes in Villena?
A: While specific public transport options directly to route starting points are not detailed, Villena is a well-connected town. For detailed information on public transport to access specific routes, it's advisable to check local bus or train schedules depending on your desired starting location.

Q: Where can I find parking when starting a road cycling route in Villena?
A: Villena, as a town, offers various parking facilities. When planning your road cycling route, consider starting points within or near the town center where public parking is typically available. For routes beginning outside the main town, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or local amenities.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Villena?
A: The road cycling experience in Villena is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 18 reviews. Over 1800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain, well-paved surfaces, and the blend of natural beauty with historical landmarks.

Q: Are there routes that pass through natural areas like the Vinalopó Valley or Sierra de Salinas?
A: Absolutely. Road cycling around Villena is defined by its natural features. Many routes traverse the Vinalopó Valley, offering views of olive groves and vineyards, and extend into the Sierra de la Villa and Sierra de Salinas, providing challenging ascents and panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pou Clar Natural Area – Biar loop from Villena&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that incorporates beautiful natural scenery.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate the Vías Verdes in Villena?
A: Yes, the "Vía Verde del Chicharra" is a prominent feature for cyclists in the region. This converted railway line offers a unique cycling experience, allowing you to explore archaeological heritage and changing landscapes. It starts near the Sanctuary of Las Virtudes and extends towards Biar, with possibilities to continue further.
tips: From its summit at 1210 meters above sea level, you will have privileged panoramic views of the entire interior of the province of Alicante and Alto Vinalopó. A fun climb with good slopes and curves with a good surface.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: While the ascent to Reconco itself is a fun climb with good slopes and curves on a well-paved surface, the overall circular route contains some segments that may be unpaved and difficult to ride for a traditional racebike. Riders should be prepared for potential changes in road surface quality, possibly encountering gravel or rougher patches.

faqs.question: How challenging is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate and requires good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of approximately 800 meters. The climb to Observatorio Forestal Reconco from Villena spans 13.5 kilometers with an average gradient of 5.1%.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the breathtaking scenery from the summit of El Reconco Summit. From its peak at 1210 meters, you'll enjoy privileged panoramic views of the entire interior of the province of Alicante and Alto Vinalopó. The surrounding region also features diverse landscapes including pine forests, vineyards, and olive and almond groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this route in Villena?
faqs.answer: Villena offers several parking options for cyclists. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in the city center or near the main access points to the route. Consider searching for public parking areas in Villena before your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as moderate with significant elevation gain and some potentially unpaved sections. It is best suited for road cyclists with good fitness and some experience, rather than beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region around Villena generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, especially during midday, and winter conditions might include colder temperatures at higher elevations, though snow is rare.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads and some potentially unpaved segments. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs due to the nature of the sport and potential traffic.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads and accessible natural areas.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will encounter sections of La Defensa del Sur, Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm, El Camí IT5TR4 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana, and Vía Verde del Xixarra, among others.

faqs.question: What should I consider packing for this ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for varied terrain, you should pack essentials for a road ride of this length. This includes sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit for punctures, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, and a helmet. Due to potential unpaved sections, consider slightly wider tires if your racebike can accommodate them, or be prepared for rougher patches.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Villena, where you will find various cafes, restaurants, and shops. Along the circular route itself, especially in the more rural and mountainous sections, services may be limited, so it's advisable to carry enough supplies.
